items.q: Why do informal settlements develop in rapidly growing cities?
items.correct: Because migration and natural increase outpace the supply of formal housing, so low-income arrivals build their own homes on marginal land without legal tenure.
items.misconception: People choose slums because they prefer not to live in proper housing.
items.random_wrong: Informal settlements are theme parks built by councils.
items.why: Understanding the housing-supply gap reframes informal settlements as a response to growth, not a choice.

items.q: Is the CBD the most densely populated part of a city?
items.correct: No — the Central Business District has the highest land values and daytime crowds, but few residents; residential density usually peaks in the zones around it.
items.misconception: Most city residents live right in the city centre.
items.random_wrong: The CBD is where cows are kept in cities.
items.why: Confusing daytime activity with residential density muddles urban land-use models.

items.q: Does urban regeneration benefit all of an area's original residents?
items.correct: Not always — regeneration brings investment, jobs and better environments, but rising rents and house prices can displace established low-income communities, a process called gentrification.
items.misconception: Improving an area automatically helps everyone who lives there.
items.random_wrong: Regeneration means demolishing the whole city.
items.why: Weighing the winners and losers of regeneration is the key evaluative skill for this theme.
It's 10 million. “1 million” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What are Rio's informal settlements called?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Favelas. “Slums” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: What percentage of world population lived in cities in 2020?
items.why: The real figure is 56 %. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
